instruments: HKMA guidance to Authorized Institutions on digital-asset custody and distribution
standing_summary: HKMA's core guidance to Authorized Institutions on digital-asset activity dates to 20 February 2024 (custody of digital assets, and sale/distribution of tokenised products), with updated custody guidance reissued 27 May 2026 expanding cyber-security, cold-wallet, and governance requirements, and an April 2025 circular permitting Authorized Institutions to offer staking services. On the retail digital-money side, HKMA completed Phase 2 of the e-HKD Pilot Programme on 28 October 2025 and stated that the immediate priority for e-HKD lies beyond retail use cases, redirecting near-term focus to wholesale payments and tokenisation-ecosystem support (branded e-HKD+). Tokenised deposits are being advanced operationally through Project Ensemble/EnsembleTX (see the Tokenization & RWA pillar) rather than through standalone tokenised-deposit guidance.

key_links.label: HKMA press release: e-HKD Pilot Programme completion and future direction (28 Oct 2025)
HKMA: Central Bank Digital Currency / e-HKD+ overview
HKMA press release: Project Ensemble new phase, EnsembleTX (13 Nov 2025)

open_items: HKMA indicated further preparatory work toward a possible future retail e-HKD would continue into H1 2026; no published outcome of that work was found yet.
No standalone HKMA guidance on tokenised deposits exists yet; the topic is currently being handled through the Project Ensemble pilot rather than a codified circular.

Several underlying HKMA circulars referenced in secondary sources (the original February 2024 custody circular, the April 2025 staking circular, and the May 2026 updated custody guidance) are hosted on HKMA's banking-sector document repository, which could not be fetched directly during this research; their contents above are corroborated by secondary legal-commentary sources and should be re-confirmed directly when that repository is reachable.
